name: Jamaican Chocolate Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 325°F (165°C).
2. Generously grease a 5-cup fluted or plain tube pan and dust lightly with flour.
3. Sift flour, baking powder, and Mixed Spice into a bowl.
4. Add margarine, superfine sugar, and eggs to the dry ingredients. Blend until well combined.
5. Mix cocoa powder with hot water in a separate bowl. Stir until smooth.
6. Add the cocoa mixture to the flour mixture. Beat for 2 minutes (or 1 minute if using an electric mixer).
7. Turn the batter into the prepared pan and bake in the preheated oven for 1-1/4 hours, or until well risen and the cake begins to shrink from the edges of the pan.
8. Carefully turn out the cake onto a wire rack and cool completely.
9. In a saucepan, combine granulated sugar and 2/3 cups water. Add cinnamon and heat gently, stirring to dissolve the sugar. Boil for 5 minutes. Remove from heat, add rum, and discard the cinnamon. Place the cake on a plate. Spoon syrup over the cake and let stand for 2 hours.
10. Sprinkle almonds evenly over the top of the cake.
11. Melt chocolate in a microwave or double boiler. Carefully spoon over the cake, spreading to give a smooth even coating. Let set for several hours.
12. Pipe whipped cream around the bottom of the cake using a pastry bag.
